content: We can hypothesize that the unified episodic-semantic memory framework rooted in human medial temporal lobe (MTL) organization defines a natural structural prior that improves latent node attribute predictability for LLM agent memory graphs. This occurs because the MTL-inspired unified account merges contextual episodic (event-specific) and general semantic (conceptual) relationships into a single graph structure, which removes spurious latent variation that would otherwise obscure the true correlations between graph topology and unobserved node attributes.
